questions.text: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, at about 58 million km.

questions.text: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: An adult has 206 bones. Babies are born with about 270 that fuse together.

questions.text: What gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO₂ and release oxygen during photosynthesis.

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, hence H₂O.

questions.text: Which scientist developed the theory of relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein published the special theory of relativity in 1905 and general relativity in 1915.

questions.text: What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?
questions.explanation: Light travels at about 299,792 km/s in a vacuum.

questions.text: What is the nucleus of an atom composed of?
questions.explanation: The atomic nucleus contains protons (positively charged) and neutrons (neutral).

questions.text: Which of these are greenhouse gases? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: CO₂ and methane (CH₄) are the two main human-produced greenhouse gases.

questions.text: Who discovered penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin by accident in 1928.

questions.text: What is the main function of red blood cells?
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin which carries oxygen from the lungs to the body's tissues.

questions.text: Which of these are gas giant planets? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: Jupiter and Saturn are gas giants, composed mainly of hydrogen and helium.

questions.text: Which molecule stores energy in cells?
questions.explanation: ATP (adenosine triphosphate) is the main energy-carrying molecule in cells.

questions.text: How does DNA differ from RNA? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: DNA is double-stranded with deoxyribose and thymine; RNA is single-stranded with ribose and uracil.

questions.text: Which chemical element is essential for life on Earth?
questions.explanation: Carbon is the fundamental element of life because it can form four covalent bonds, enabling complex molecules.

questions.text: What is the Higgs boson?

choices.text: A particle that gives mass to other particles

questions.explanation: The Higgs boson, discovered at CERN in 2012, gives elementary particles mass through the Higgs field.

questions.text: In which year did the French Revolution begin?
questions.explanation: The storming of the Bastille on July 14, 1789 marked the beginning of the French Revolution.

questions.text: In which year did humans first walk on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969 with Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin.

questions.text: Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar?
questions.explanation: Julius Caesar was a general and dictator of the Roman Republic, laying the foundations for the Roman Empire.

questions.text: Who was the first Emperor of China?
questions.explanation: Qin Shi Huang unified China and became its first emperor in 221 BCE.

questions.text: In which year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 with Germany's surrender in May and Japan's in September.

questions.text: What event triggered World War I?
questions.explanation: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria in Sarajevo on June 28, 1914 triggered World War I.

questions.text: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, marking the end of the Cold War.

questions.text: Which countries were part of the Allies in WWII? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The Allies included the United States, the United Kingdom, the Soviet Union, and Free France.

questions.text: Who discovered America in 1492?
questions.explanation: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas on October 12, 1492, under the Spanish Crown.

questions.text: Which treaty ended World War I?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles was signed on June 28, 1919, officially ending World War I.

questions.text: What were causes of the French Revolution? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The French Revolution of 1789 was caused by social inequality under the three estates system and the state's financial crisis.

questions.text: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George Washington served as the first US President from 1789 to 1797.

questions.text: When was the United Nations founded?
questions.explanation: The United Nations was founded on October 24, 1945, after World War II.

questions.text: Which civilizations existed in Mesoamerica? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The Maya and Aztec civilizations flourished in Mesoamerica. The Inca were in South America.

questions.text: Who was the last pharaoh of Egypt?
questions.explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of Ptolemaic Egypt before it became a Roman province in 30 BCE.

questions.text: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris has been the capital of France since the 10th century.

questions.text: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile is traditionally considered the longest river at about 6,650 km.

questions.text: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest, covering about 165 million km².

questions.text: What is the tallest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest stands at 8,849 meters above sea level.

questions.text: What is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara covers about 9.2 million km² across North Africa.

questions.text: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra has been Australia's capital since 1913.

questions.text: Which strait separates Europe from Africa?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Spain from Morocco, connecting the Mediterranean to the Atlantic.

questions.text: Which is the most populous country in the world?
questions.explanation: India surpassed China in 2023 to become the most populous country with over 1.4 billion people.

questions.text: Which countries does the equator cross? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The equator crosses Brazil and Indonesia, among other countries in South America, Africa, and Asia.

questions.text: What is the largest freshwater lake by volume?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal in Siberia is the largest freshwater lake by volume, containing about 20% of the world's surface freshwater.

questions.text: How many countries are in the European Union in 2024?
questions.explanation: The European Union has 27 member states as of 2024 after Brexit.

questions.text: What percentage of Earth is covered by water?
questions.explanation: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water, mostly in the form of oceans.

questions.text: What is the lowest point on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench in the Pacific Ocean is the deepest point on Earth's crust, at about 11,000 m below sea level.

questions.text: Which countries are entirely in the Southern Hemisphere? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: Australia, Argentina, and South Africa are entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.

questions.text: What is the longest mountain range in the world?
questions.explanation: The Andes in South America are the longest mountain range at about 7,000 km.

questions.text: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci between 1503 and 1519.

questions.text: Which instrument has 88 keys?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys (52 white and 36 black).

questions.text: Who invented the printing press in Europe?
questions.explanation: Johannes Gutenberg invented the movable-type printing press around 1450.

questions.text: How many plays did Shakespeare write?
questions.explanation: Shakespeare wrote 37 plays (39 counting collaborations).

questions.text: What are the primary colors in painting? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The subtractive primary colors are red, yellow, and blue.

questions.text: What is the best-selling book worldwide after the Bible?
questions.explanation: The Little Prince by Antoine de Saint-Exupéry is the best-selling book after the Bible, with over 200 million copies.

questions.text: Who composed the Ninth Symphony?
questions.explanation: Beethoven composed the Ninth Symphony, famous for its 'Ode to Joy'.

questions.text: Who said 'I think, therefore I am'?
questions.explanation: 'Cogito, ergo sum' is a famous philosophical statement by René Descartes.

questions.text: Who delivered the 'I have a dream' speech?
questions.explanation: Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his famous 'I have a dream' speech on August 28, 1963 in Washington D.C.

questions.text: Where is the Louvre Museum located?
questions.explanation: The Louvre Museum in Paris is the largest museum in the world.

questions.text: What is the Nobel Prize?

choices.text: An international award in several fields

questions.explanation: The Nobel Prize is an international award created by Alfred Nobel, given in Physics, Chemistry, Medicine, Literature, and Peace.

questions.text: Which ancient wonder was located in Babylon?
questions.explanation: The Hanging Gardens of Babylon are one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

questions.text: Who founded the Red Cross?
questions.explanation: Henri Dunant founded the Red Cross in 1863 after witnessing the horrors of the Battle of Solferino.

questions.text: What are the fundamental rights in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The 1948 Universal Declaration guarantees the right to life, freedom of expression, and education, among others.

questions.text: What year was the United Nations founded?
questions.explanation: The United Nations was founded on October 24, 1945.

questions.text: What color is the leader's jersey in the Tour de France?
questions.explanation: The Tour de France leader wears the yellow jersey (maillot jaune).

questions.text: Where were the ancient Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The ancient Olympic Games were held at Olympia, Greece, starting in 776 BCE.

questions.text: Which country won the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: France won the 2018 World Cup, defeating Croatia 4-2 in the final.

questions.text: What surface is the French Open played on?
questions.explanation: The French Open at Roland Garros is played on clay courts.

questions.text: What is the official marathon distance?
questions.explanation: The marathon is 42.195 km (26.2 miles), standardized in 1921.

questions.text: Which sports are part of the Summer Olympics? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: Athletics and football are traditional Olympic sports; windsurfing was introduced in 1984.

questions.text: Who holds the 100m world record?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt holds the 100m record at 9.58 seconds, set in 2009.

questions.text: Which team has the most NBA championships?
questions.explanation: The Boston Celtics hold the record with 18 NBA championships.

questions.text: How many players are on a football (soccer) team on the field?
questions.explanation: A football team has 11 players on the field, including one goalkeeper.

questions.text: When were the first modern Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens in 1896, founded by Pierre de Coubertin.

questions.text: Which country hosted the 2023 Rugby World Cup?
questions.explanation: France hosted the 2023 Rugby World Cup.

questions.text: Which are the Grand Slam tennis tournaments? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The four Grand Slam tournaments are the Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open.

questions.text: Which footballer has won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards, a record.

questions.text: What sport uses a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton is played with a shuttlecock (made of feathers or synthetic material) and lightweight rackets.

questions.text: Who won the 2024 Tour de France?
questions.explanation: Tadej Pogačar won the 2024 Tour de France for the third time.

questions.text: Who played Jack Dawson in Titanic?
questions.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio played Jack Dawson in James Cameron's Titanic (1997).

questions.text: Which band performed 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?
questions.explanation: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' is a song by Queen released in 1975.

questions.text: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (unadjusted)?
questions.explanation: Avatar (2009) by James Cameron is the highest-grossing film with over $2.9 billion.

questions.text: Which are main characters from Friends? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The six main characters in Friends are Rachel, Ross, Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe.

questions.text: Who directed 'Inception'?
questions.explanation: Christopher Nolan directed Inception (2010) starring Leonardo DiCaprio.

questions.text: Which Nintendo handheld console popularized portable gaming?
questions.explanation: The Game Boy (1989) revolutionized portable gaming and sold over 118 million units.

questions.text: Which video game franchise features Mario?
questions.explanation: Mario is the protagonist of Nintendo's Super Mario series.

questions.text: Who sang 'Imagine'?
questions.explanation: John Lennon wrote and performed 'Imagine', released in 1971.

questions.text: Which are the Hogwarts houses in Harry Potter? (multiple answers)
questions.explanation: The four Hogwarts houses are Gryffindor, Slytherin, Hufflepuff, and Ravenclaw.

questions.text: Which actor played Iron Man in the MCU?
questions.explanation: Robert Downey Jr. played Tony Stark / Iron Man from 2008 to 2019 in the MCU.

questions.text: How many rings of power were forged in The Lord of the Rings?
questions.explanation: 20 rings of power were forged: 3 for Elves, 7 for Dwarves, 9 for Men, and 1 One Ring.

questions.text: Who created the TV series Breaking Bad?
questions.explanation: Vince Gilligan created Breaking Bad (2008-2013), the acclaimed series about a chemistry teacher turned meth producer.

questions.text: Which Pixar film features a balloon-filled flying house?
questions.explanation: Up (2009) follows Carl Fredricksen who uses thousands of balloons to fly his house to South America.

questions.text: Which artist has sold the most albums in history?
questions.explanation: The Beatles are the best-selling artists in history with about 600 million albums sold.

questions.text: Which video game series features Link and Zelda?
questions.explanation: Link and Princess Zelda are the main characters in Nintendo's The Legend of Zelda series.
